messages.content: Several risk write-ups may describe the same underlying risk. Pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) and fold any UNIQUE early-warning indicators from the others into it. If they are genuinely different risks, keep the strongest as exemplar anyway only when they truly overlap — otherwise return the highest-scored id with no folded indicators and note they are distinct.

content: {"keepId":"risk.definition-scopedown.colo-scope-geography-misallocation","rationale":"Risks 1 and 2 are the same underlying risk: apportioning all-segment TAM (including the 29% enterprise on-prem) by colocation MW misplaces ~29% of the market across countries. Risk 2 is the sharper exemplar — it ties the correlated, same-direction scope errors (DE 0.50 vs 0.42, CH 0.08 vs 0.13, PL 0.07 vs 0.10) explicitly to the operator-first Frankfurt/Amsterdam channel and the serviceableFactor, adding a concrete decision consequence that Risk 1 only gestures at. Risk 1's unique early-warning signals (national installed-load figures diverging from colo shares; Statista-vs-CBRE scope reconciliation) are worth folding in. Risk 3 is a genuinely DIFFERENT risk: it does not dispute the geography apportionment's segment/scope basis but attacks the euros-per-MW constant-intensity assumption (NL MW carry less discrete-PDU content because of hyperscale self-build). Different mechanism, different signals — not folded.","foldedIndicators":[{"signal":"Statista vs CBRE scope reconciliation for 2025/2026 vintages","where":"CBRE Europe Data Centres quarterly vs Statista colocation-by-country table updates","threshold":"Gap between summed Statista country colo and CBRE European totals persisting or widening","updates":"increases"}]}
